title: papaver pyrenaicum subsp. aurantiacum
intro: Papaver pyrenaicum subsp. aurantiacum, commonly known as the orange poppy or Pyrenean poppy, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the poppy family (Papaveraceae). This plant is known for its bright orange flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Papaver pyrenaicum subsp. aurantiacum include relief from pain, inflammation, and anxiety, as well as improved sleep quality. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a variety of health issues, including digestive problems and menstrual cramps.
